CPUs are having a moment.  #Nvidia launched a standalone CPU. #Arm made its first chip in 35 years. #Intel & #AMD are raising prices amid a supply crunch.  What's behind it: Agentic AI needs far more CPU than anyone planned for — driving a structural shift in CPU:GPU ratios

Memory prices keep climbing: DRAM is forecast to rise 58–63% QoQ in 2Q26, while NAND flash may surge 70–75%. When will the supply-demand imbalance ease?More analysis from #TrendForce:  https:// buff.ly/NBcmN5k

Recent media reports claiming NVIDIA has exclusively selected Samsung and SK hynix for its HBM4 supply chain—supposedly sidelining Micron—are inaccurate. TrendForce reports that all three memory giants remain integral to the upcoming Vera Rubin architecture. Samsung plans to

#SanDisk announced that the company has secured long-term #SSD supply agreements of up to five years, featuring increasing volume commitments and a hybrid pricing structure that combines fixed and variable components. These agreements are designed to ensure demand visibility and

TSMC's #CoPoS is moving fast, and the opportunities for Taiwan suppliers are taking shape. CoPoS replaces traditional round glass carrier with a square panel format, pushing utilization rates above 75%. More on glass material development:  https:// pse.is/977q8c
#Intel Foundry's turnaround may be coming sooner than expected — with big tech now reportedly betting on its 18A node and advanced packaging.
#HBM prices may surge in 2027. HBM4 negotiations are underway, with suppliers holding the upper hand amid tight DRAM supply and rising costs.More analysis from #TrendForce:   https:// buff.ly/EM8TOV3

#HBF is emerging as the next-gen memory for AI inference, and the equipment race is already underway — Hanmi Semiconductor reportedly set to deliver the first batch of HBF TC bonders to customers in 2H26. More:  https:// buff.ly/8LliD7m

Power semiconductors, particularly SiC and GaN devices, are expected to play an increasingly important role in future HVDC power architectures as AI data centers contend with immense power consumption. Although the South Korean government has launched a $494 million R&D
#Intel is set to showcase 18A-P, while #TSMC prepares to debut A16 with its first Super Power Rail architecture at VLSI. What was once a roadmap race is now turning into a head-to-head showdown.
